hard.ais_why:
• Per the 11 May 2026 rep note: 'confirmed by email- shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox- need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here.' Across 3,282 chars of NextStep spanning Jan 2025 to May 2026 ‚Äî covering RFP, demos, onsite presentations and shortlisting ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, or autonomous despite Merlin, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI being in product scope.
• On 5 Mar 2026: 'Onsite presentation/demo for 3 hours scheduled for 10th March.' On 12 Mar 2026: 'Completed onsite demo, they have a few more vendors to review.' On 12 Dec 2025: 'CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January, looking to decide on a vendor by April and start implementation.' The decision framework is CFO-led, vendor-comparison-driven (vs GEP/iValua/Coupa/Ariba) with no AI-specific evaluation criterion captured.
• Confidence is Medium: heavy AI product loadout (3 AI SKUs) signals Zycus positioning, but the buyer-side dialogue captured across 16 months is pure traditional S2P RFP ‚Äî onsite demos, BAFO, 'hands on sandbox', local reference. The 4/10 score reflects AI in scope without AI in conversation; would move up only if hands-on sandbox produces AI-specific buyer engagement.

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is down-selected to the final two, has passed both reference checks (Nissan and TataPlay, completed 22-30 Jun), and TTI has asked (1 Jul) for revised S2C-only pricing plus a phased implementation plan and RACI, but the 30 Jul close cannot absorb the CFO scope decision due the week of 13 Jul, business-case approval, and contract execution. The deal is healthy and engaged; the date will slip 60-90 days to the buyer's own H2 solution-design kickoff.
north_star_verdict.math: Opp created 20 Jan 2025 (529 days old). Stage Shortlisted since at least 11 May 2026 (56+ days in stage). Close date 30 Jul 2026 is 24 days out and was pulled in on 30 Jun (2026-08-30 -> 2026-07-30) after earlier moves (23 Jun -> 30 Jun -> 30 Aug). Buyer's own path: CFO scope decision week of 13 Jul, then revised S2C pricing, business-case/parent approval, contract, then solution-design kickoff the buyer already deferred to H2 2026. Remaining steps cannot complete by 30 Jul; a 60-90 day forward slip is required.
evidence: Next Step 1 Jul 2026: 'likely to now move from S2P to S2C (P2P will come early new year). Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa. Now they want S2C revised pricing and implementation estimates/timelines. They are meeting CFO week of 13th July to decide on scope. This is all coming from the risk they feel moving from ECC to S4Hana, want proper and revised RACI, project plan, like all the other vendors gave them (we didn't).'
Next Step 30 Jun 2026: 'Tej Ram responded to Carl, they have completed references and are going through final stages of process.'
Task 22 Jun 2026 (email from Duncan/Tej): 'We have completed both the Nissan and Tata Play reference calls. The team will now review their notes and identify any additional questions.'

Next Step 11 May 2026: 'confirmed by email, shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox, need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here.'
Field history: CloseDate 2026-08-30 -> 2026-07-30 on 30 Jun 2026 (by Carl Kimball).

RFP briefing call 12 Jan 2026 (Duncan Tennyson): 'we will need to put a pause in business case... we're really probably not looking at starting any sort of solution design work moving into implementation until July.'
Engagement pulse: last verified Salesforce activity 1 Jul 2026 (buyer email received, genuine two-way touch). Deal is LIVE.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is modernising Source-to-Pay across the Australia and New Zealand region ahead of a SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA migration, to bring spend under management, enforce PO-against-contract compliance and guided buying. Tej Ram sized the scale on the 12 Jan 2026 RFP briefing: roughly 110 million per annum flowing through POs and about 130 million per annum through a non-PO invoice process, with 10 power users and about 200 business users across ANZ. No ROI percentage, savings target, or cycle-time benefit has been quantified by the buyer, so the value case is sized by spend volume but not yet by benefit. Quantifying the automation and savings impact with TTI's finance lead ahead of the 13 Jul CFO scope meeting is the move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is the Melbourne-based CFO who mandated the tender and controls costing approval: on the 12 Dec 2025 call the buyer said 'we've got some instructions from up top from our CFO that he wants to tender out' and 'the CFO is based here in Melbourne... he interacts with our parent company in USA quite a bit.' TTI is 'meeting CFO week of 13th July to decide on scope' (Next Step 1 Jul 2026), confirming the CFO owns the scope-and-budget decision, with final costings also routed to the US parent for approval. The CFO has never been on a Zycus call (a conflict-of-interest bar was cited during the RFP on 10 Feb), so Zycus has no direct access to the budget owner. Salesforce names no CFO contact; securing an introduction right after the 13 Jul decision is the ranked move.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit from the RFP briefing and the reference/shortlist stage: completeness of written response, technical and functional fit against the requirements workbook, a strong implementation program with phasing and resourcing guidance, referenceable ANZ customers, and commercial terms (licence, implementation, support/SLAs). Duncan Tennyson stated on 12 Jan 2026 'we're very interested in the implementation program... what does a successful implementation look like, phasing, sequence.' By the down-select the criteria sharpened to a local reference (Nissan and TataPlay delivered), hands-on sandbox validation, and a phased, de-risked S2C implementation plan with a RACI and project plan, which the buyer flagged on 1 Jul competitors provided and Zycus did not. Integration approach for the ECC6-then-S/4HANA landscape is a weighted technical criterion owned by Duncan.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a CFO-mandated formal RFP run on the Gartner BuySmart platform: written questionnaires (350 questions), a 3-hour onsite demo (10 Mar), BAFO/revised pricing (Apr), a shortlist to two on 11 May, reference calls (Nissan and TataPlay, completed 22 Jun), then commercials, sandbox testing and an integration deep-dive. The buyer-side committee is clear from the call sequence: Duncan Tennyson project-manages and owns the IT/integration side, Tej Ram owns procurement transformation, Sonia Gopalan is the group procurement manager, with the Melbourne CFO holding the scope-and-budget gate (13 Jul meeting) and the US parent approving final costings. Approval is multi-layer (local CFO plus parent sign-off), which is the primary reason the 30 Jul close date is not credible.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partially visible: the RFP required vendors to upload a contract template and flag clauses in conflict with TTI's legal and contractual requirements (RFP section 4), and Duncan sent TTI's terms and conditions from the Gartner BuySmart tool for Zycus legal review (email 19 May 2026, Adam committed to have legal respond within end of week). Procurement runs through Gartner BuySmart, with pricing and commercials handled off-platform by email. IT is separately gathering InfoSec/security requirements (12 Dec 2025 catch-up). No MSA/SOW redline stage, signature authority, or contract-expiration forcing function has been reached; the S/4HANA migration timing is the dependency. A mutual close plan naming the legal, InfoSec, and parent-approval steps would create visibility on the paper path.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed operational pain is a legacy SAP ECC6 landscape mid-migration to S/4HANA, which creates integration risk and a 'moving target' Duncan flagged directly, alongside a need to get spend under management, enforce PO-against-contract compliance, and modernise guided buying, tail-spend and invoicing (currently on SCM/escrow OCR). Tej Ram owns the procurement-transformation pain; Duncan Tennyson owns the ECC6/S/4HANA integration and change-management risk ('want proper and revised RACI, project plan', 1 Jul 2026). Inferred pain is adoption difficulty for roughly 200 occasional business users, which is exactly why Merlin Intake inside Microsoft Teams was positioned. The pain is board-sanctioned: a business case was built for CFO and board approval before the RFP was issued.

sources: Salesforce Contact Role (Tej Ram, Evaluator, Primary)
champion.narrative: Tej Ram (Manager - Procurement & Transformation), the primary Salesforce contact and Evaluator, is an active operational champion: he project-manages the procurement side, keeps Zycus informed of timeline shifts, responded directly to Carl Kimball on 29-30 Jun, and offered to set up a meeting to review the updated commercials (1 Jul 2026). His rating is developing rather than strong because he has not secured direct CFO engagement for Zycus, has not introduced Zycus to the US parent stakeholders who approve global costings, and the buyer flagged that Zycus fell short on the RACI/project plan competitors provided, a gap a strong champion would have pre-empted. Converting his advocacy into a CFO introduction immediately after the 13 Jul scope decision is the missing step.

competition.narrative: The field was named early and narrowed on evidence: the Jan 2026 RFP went to 3-4 vendors and the buyer referenced Coupa, iValua and Ariba (Next Step 13 Jan 2026); by 11 May 2026 TTI down-selected to two, naming GEP and iValua as the 'strong threats.' GEP is the single live current threat as a co-finalist with no disqualifying evidence. Coupa has weakened, the buyer noting the S2C scope shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa' (1 Jul 2026), and Ariba has been silent since January. The wedge is the full suite on one codebase with Merlin AI differentiation (Merlin Intake in Microsoft Teams, AP OCR, agentic tail-spend negotiation), which Zycus should convert into a de-risked phased S2C plan and RACI sharper than GEP's to hold the down-select lead.

deal_movement.summary: Live and progressing: since the 11 May down-select to two, references (Nissan, TataPlay) were completed and passed by 22-30 Jun, and on 1 Jul the buyer pivoted scope from S2P to S2C-only and asked for revised pricing and a phased implementation plan. Last genuine two-way touch was 1 Jul 2026 (buyer email). Close date was pulled in to 30 Jul on 30 Jun.

items.change: Down-selected to the final two vendors alongside GEP and iValua

competitive_position.summary: GEP is the single strongest current threat as of Jul 2026: named alongside Zycus as a shortlist-to-two finalist and a 'strong threat' on 11 May 2026, with no evidence of elimination since and the buyer explicitly citing that competitors (including GEP) supplied a RACI and project plan Zycus did not. iValua was the other named 'strong threat' in May but has not recurred and has faded. Coupa has weakened, the buyer stating on 1 Jul 2026 that the S2C scope shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa.' SAP Ariba was in the initial Jan 2026 field but silent since, effectively dormant. Recency-weighted rank: GEP (high, active finalist) > Coupa (low, faded) > iValua (low, faded) > SAP Ariba (dormant).

The AI positioning is Zycus-led, not buyer-pulled; a hands-on sandbox is the chance to convert it.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of two finalists that has passed both reference checks, but the buyer has narrowed scope to S2C and asked for the implementation RACI competitors already gave; the deal is winnable if we out-plan GEP on implementation and reach the CFO before the 13 Jul scope decision.

win_position.text: Why this number: Shortlisted caps confidence near 70; the final-two down-select plus passed Nissan and TataPlay references earn a strong position, but it holds in the mid-60s because GEP is still a live co-finalist and the CFO scope decision is unmade.
Zycus is down-selected to two versus GEP and iValua (11 May) and the S2C shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa' (1 Jul).
GEP remains a live 'strong threat' finalist with no elimination evidence, and the buyer said competitors gave a RACI and project plan Zycus did not (1 Jul).
Scope narrowed from S2P to S2C with P2P deferred, a cost-defensive signal that shrinks the deal and can reopen the field if full scope returns.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is real: references completed and passed by 22-30 Jun and the buyer moved to 'final stages of process' (30 Jun).
The buyer named a concrete forcing date, the CFO scope meeting the week of 13 Jul, and requested revised pricing and phasing (1 Jul).
Close date was pulled in to 30 Jul on 30 Jun yet the remaining CFO, business-case and contract steps cannot complete by then, so a 60-90 day slip is coming.

customer_commitment.text: Tej Ram is an active champion who responded to Carl 29 Jun-1 Jul and offered a review meeting, and the buyer invested in two reference calls.
Commitment is capped because the CFO economic buyer has never met Zycus and final costings still need US-parent approval.

deal_risk.text: The 30 Jul close is not credible: the 13 Jul CFO scope decision, revised pricing, business case and contract cannot land in 24 days.
Zycus lacks direct access to the budget owner and is exposed on the implementation RACI that GEP already provided.

close_plan.evidence: No mutual close plan; buyer path (13 Jul CFO scope, pricing, business case, parent approval, contract) cannot fit the 30 Jul date.

executive_access.evidence: Melbourne CFO who owns scope and budget has never been on a Zycus call; access blocked during the RFP as a conflict of interest.

customer_preference.evidence: S2C scope shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa' (1 Jul) and Zycus is one of two finalists after passed references, but no explicit buyer praise and GEP is still a named strong threat.

business_case.evidence: Spend sized at ~110M per annum via POs and ~130M via non-PO invoices with 10 power and ~200 business users (12 Jan), but no ROI, savings percentage or cycle-time benefit quantified by the buyer.

reasons.summary: Scope shrinking vs prior — On 1 Jul 2026 the buyer signalled a move from S2P to S2C-only with P2P pushed to the new year, driven by S/4HANA migration risk; a cost-defensive, phased posture that shrinks scope and must be managed. Buyer likely getting defensive on cost or implementation/integration (phased over big-bang) — watch.

commentary.win_position: Win 59 is carried by momentum 68 vs stage-expected 56; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — On 1 Jul 2026 the buyer signalled a move from S2P to S2C-only with P2P pushed to the new year, driven by S/4HANA….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): Next Step carries 6 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 43 is anchored on the stage win (59), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Techtronic Industries Company Limited — Shortlisted · $220K · closes 30 Jul
cro_panel.intro: Zycus is one of two finalists that has passed both reference checks, but the buyer has narrowed scope to S2C and asked for the implementation RACI competitors already gave; the deal is winnable if we out-plan GEP on implementation and reach the CFO before the 13 Jul scope decision.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.

bullets.text: Why this number: Shortlisted caps confidence near 70; the final-two down-select plus passed Nissan and TataPlay references earn a strong position, but it holds in the mid-60s because GEP is still a live co-finalist and the CFO scope decision is unmade.
Zycus is down-selected to two versus GEP and iValua (11 May) and the S2C shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa' (1 Jul).
GEP remains a live 'strong threat' finalist with no elimination evidence, and the buyer said competitors gave a RACI and project plan Zycus did not (1 Jul).
Scope narrowed from S2P to S2C with P2P deferred, a cost-defensive signal that shrinks the deal and can reopen the field if full scope returns.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 59.

bullets.text: Momentum is real: references completed and passed by 22-30 Jun and the buyer moved to 'final stages of process' (30 Jun).
The buyer named a concrete forcing date, the CFO scope meeting the week of 13 Jul, and requested revised pricing and phasing (1 Jul).
Close date was pulled in to 30 Jul on 30 Jun yet the remaining CFO, business-case and contract steps cannot complete by then, so a 60-90 day slip is coming.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.

bullets.text: The 30 Jul close is not credible: the 13 Jul CFO scope decision, revised pricing, business case and contract cannot land in 24 days.
Zycus lacks direct access to the budget owner and is exposed on the implementation RACI that GEP already provided.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: GEP is the single strongest current threat as of Jul 2026: named alongside Zycus as a shortlist-to-two finalist and a 'strong threat' on 11 May 2026, with no evidence of elimination since and the buyer explicitly citing that competitors…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Tej Ram is an active champion who responded to Carl 29 Jun-1 Jul and offered a review meeting, and the buyer invested in two reference calls.
Commitment is capped because the CFO economic buyer has never met Zycus and final costings still need US-parent approval.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Send revised S2C-only commercials with phased costing before the CFO scope meeting. (by 10 Jul)
Deliver a phased S2C implementation plan with RACI tied to the S/4HANA timeline. (by 11 Jul)
Ask Tej to arrange a CFO alignment call on S2C scope right after the 13 Jul decision. (by 17 Jul)

calls_omitted.reason: No transcript available in the provided record

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120d; field history through 1 Jul 2026
gaps: Economic-buyer CFO is unnamed in Salesforce and never on a call; identity known only by role (Melbourne CFO).
No quantified ROI/savings benefit case stated by the buyer.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'SAP ECC')
